title: resp.bytes() can buffer unbounded data if Content-Length absent
description: In fetch_card() (src/cards.rs lines 99-105), the Content-Length pre-check on line 93 only fires when the header is present. If the server omits Content-Length (chunked transfer encoding), resp.bytes().await buffers the entire response into memory before the body.len() check on line 103. A malicious or misconfigured server could stream hundreds of MB before the 10-second timeout kills the connection. Fix: use a streaming approach — read in chunks and abort when cumulative size exceeds 1MB. For example, use resp.chunk() in a loop or wrap the response body in a take() adapter.
